title: Electron's resource and size cost
description: Each app bundles a full Chromium and Node runtime, so binaries are large and every window carries a renderer process's memory overhead.
body: Use when reasoning about app footprint and performance. Because Electron embeds Chromium and Node, installers are tens of megabytes minimum and each window spawns a renderer process with its own memory; many windows or heavy pages multiply RAM use. Do not expect a native-app-sized footprint. Actions: minimize windows, reuse renderers where sensible, prune dependencies and assets, and lazy-load heavy features. Validation signals include measured per-window memory and a justified bundle size. Risk boundary: spawning many heavyweight renderers can exhaust memory on modest machines — budget process count and memory deliberately rather than opening windows freely.
